name: Champagne Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. If using optional ingredients, see instructions at the end of this recipe.
2. Cut chicken into 4-inch wide slices.
3. Melt butter and olive oil in a large heavy skillet.
4. Saute the chicken for 4 to 5 minutes until browned on all sides, then remove it from the pan and keep warm.
5. Deglaze the pan with the champagne, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
6. Add the heavy cream to the pan, followed by the tarragon. Reduce the sauce for two minutes over medium heat.
7. Return the chicken to the pan and gently bring it back to a simmer.
8. Remove the dish to a serving platter. Serve hot with fresh vegetables or over rice or pasta (such as fettucini) or a side of Fettucini Alfredo.
Instructions for optional ingredients:
1. Pound each chicken breast half between sheets of waxed paper until about 1/8-inch thick.
2. Saute the shallots in butter until tender.
3. Divide the shallots equally and place them on each chicken breast half, spreading evenly over each piece.
4. Place one slice of Prosciutto on each chicken breast half, fold them over, and secure with toothpicks.
5. Continue sauteing the chicken for 4 to 5 minutes on each side in the remaining butter and olive oil until cooked through (it should spring back when lightly pressed with a finger).
6. Follow the sauce instructions as outlined above.
